macro_summary: Markets traded flat on Monday as hawkish Fed commentary offset easing trade war fears. The US 10-year yield eased slightly to 4.45%, while WTI climbed 1.6% to $91.14 on renewed Hormuz transit friction. A new US-China chip loophole closure added fresh uncertainty to the semis complex.
commodities_note: WTI recovered 1.6% to $91.14 as Iran's 'controlled uncertainty' strategy kept the Hormuz risk premium elevated despite ceasefire draft talks. Gold was flat at $4,522.70, failing to attract safe-haven bids, while the DXY slipped 0.1% to 98.97.

thesis_highlights: Blackwell Ultra ramp + Vera Rubin sampling are the multi-leg bull thesis — no confirmed revenue from Vera Rubin yet.

NVDA fiscal year offset from CY: Q4 FY2026 print = $68.1B (+73%), Q1 FY2027 guide = $78B (Feb 26 2026 earnings call). Vera Rubin samples shipping but contribute no booked revenue — Blackwell + Hopper still drive the print.

writeup.headline: Hard-blocked to CAUTION at +5.9% above the 50-day as China loophole closure adds risk.
writeup.what_to_do: The US move to close an AI chip export loophole for Chinese subsidiaries (per CNBC, May 31) adds fresh supply-chain risk to NVDA's already extended technicals. R:R has collapsed to 0.5:1. Calibration warning: CAUTION preceded a >+5% rally in 8/15 cases — the stock frequently underprices structural AI demand — but +5.9% above the SMA50 is a mechanical block that cannot be ignored. Reassess on a pullback to $199.35.
writeup.tight_upside_disclosure: Nearest resistance at $212.17 (0.5% above) — headline R:R only materialises once that level breaks on above-average volume. Any upgrade to WATCH or BUY is conditional on $212.17 breaking on above-average volume.
writeup.valuation_judgment: Trades at a 42% discount to the semis cluster median — the cheapest name in the group, but 42% below cluster peers reflects the cyclical risk repricing.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if Blackwell Ultra (B300) ramp (Q2-Q3 2026) is delayed, cancelled, or underwhelms market expectations.

singapore.summary: DBS catches a bid on its wealth expansion plan, but all three banks remain hard-blocked by overbought RSIs and extensions. With US10Y at 4.45%, the dual-channel NIM compression is fully intact, and the cluster awaits a catalyst to break the stalemate.
singapore.key_development: DBS announced 18 new Asian wealth centres by end-2027 (per Reuters, June 1), a franchise play that does not shift the rate-driven NIM headwind.

semiconductors.summary: Extreme divergence defines the semis cluster. Memory names (MU, SK Hynix) continue their parabolic run on AI demand, while ASIC and foundry names consolidate. SOXX is up 26.5% in a month, but 12 of 14 names are hard-blocked on extension, leaving no clean entry. AVGO's June 4 earnings are the next binary catalyst for the group.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically impossible to enter.
semiconductors.key_development: AVGO reports in 3 days — a beat on AI ASIC backlog would validate the custom silicon thesis; a miss triggers a -9% reset.

big_tech.summary: MSFT and AMZN lead the cluster higher on AI infrastructure capex narratives, but both are extended and hard-blocked. GOOG's TPU dominance keeps it relevant, while PLTR's defense AI surge is pricing in perfection at 75x forward PE. All four names are on CAUTION.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally mixed — capex confirmed, but credit-circularity risk and extreme valuations persist.
big_tech.key_development: Copilot 365 expansion to Accenture's 743k employees (per Insider Monkey, June 1) is a major enterprise win for MSFT.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Direct AVGO competitor in the custom ASIC space — hyperscaler ASIC spend trajectory is the shared revenue signal.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if AVGO's June 4 earnings validate custom ASIC demand growth above $12B consensus.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA's matched $2B photonics investment makes COHR the key partner alongside LITE for AI datacenter optics.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist on a pullback to the 200-day moving average — photonics demand is structural but the stock has run.

interconnected.name: Dell Technologies
interconnected.reason: AI-optimized server revenue surged 757% YoY (per Yahoo Finance, May 29), directly mirroring NVDA/AMD GPU demand.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if NVDA's Q1 FY2027 guide of $78B pulls datacenter capex forward, lifting Dell's AI server backlog.

interconnected.name: Super Micro Computer
interconnected.reason: Sympathy play on Dell's AI server boom (per Yahoo Finance, May 29); NVDA GPU adoption drives SMCI's rack-scale server demand.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor for a pullback to the 100-day moving average — governance concerns persist but AI server demand is undeniable.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking spend is the direct read-through from NVDA's Spectrum-X and Broadcom's switch portfolios.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Broadcom's June 4 earnings confirm networking revenue above $4B — signaling enterprise AI switch adoption.

active_risks: Hormuz Leverage: Iran adopts 'controlled uncertainty' strategy, keeping a stranglehold on global oil transit (per Oil & Gas 360, May 29).
US-China Chip Controls: US closes loophole on AI chip exports to Chinese subsidiaries outside China (per CNBC, May 31).
China Retaliation: New outbound investment rules and technology export restrictions take effect July 1 (per WSJ, June 1).
Fed Rate Hike Risk: Fed officials openly discuss rate hikes as PCE inflation hits 3.8% (per Reuters, May 29).
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil recovers to $91 as Hormuz friction persists, but ceasefire talks cap the upside. Chip stocks face fresh uncertainty from the US-China loophole closure, weighing on NVDA and AMD. The US10Y at 4.45% reflects a market pricing in 'higher for longer' Fed policy.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US closes loophole allowing Nvidia AI chip shipments to Chinese firms outside China (per CNBC, May 31). China publishes sweeping new trade restrictions (per WSJ, June 1).
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash and wait for a pullback in semis or a resolution in the Middle East before deploying capital. The extreme extension across the watchlist means patience is the only edge right now.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 19 of 26 names are hard-blocked on technical extension — extreme concentration risk in an overbought market kept alive by AI euphoria.

watch_for_entry.note: RSI reset to 45 below the SMA50. Headline R:R 13.8:1 distorted; sizing R:R 1.3:1 to $800.56 structural support. Not yet robust enough for ACCUMULATE, but the nearest actionable semis setup.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Wait for a bounce above $855 with above-average volume.

watch_for_entry.note: Pinned at the 50-day at $7.56. Sizing R:R 1.3:1 to $7.23 structural support. ADR/China delisting risk is a standing concern.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter $7.47-7.55 on a support test with volume. Stop below $7.23.

hold_no_action.note: Upgraded to HOLD as RSI normalised to 62 and extension trimmed to 2.6%. Upgrades to WATCH on a SGD 38.73 reclaim with volume.
Consolidating near the $213 support zone after the post-IPO sell-off. Wait for the market to absorb the early lockup unwind.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=11) spanning chop, trend_down, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
